description: Added a third hidden layer (`t_dense3: Linear(256→256)`) to the time embedding MLP, giving the model a deeper 3-layer projection (sinusoidal→256→256→256) to better condition on the diffusion timestep at minimal parameter cost (~65k extra params).

Added a 4th hidden layer (`t_dense4: Linear(256→256)`) to the time embedding MLP, deepening the timestep projection from 3 to 4 layers (sinusoidal→256→256→256→256), following up on the successful 3-layer experiment which gave −0.004 improvement at minimal parameter cost (~65k extra params).

Added a 5th hidden layer (`t_dense5: Linear(256→256)`) to the time embedding MLP, extending the projection to sinusoidal→256→256→256→256→256, continuing the pattern of deeper time conditioning that yielded consistent gains at ~65k extra params per layer.

Increased `time_emb_dim` from 64 to 128 while keeping the MLP hidden dimension fixed at 256 (instead of scaling as `time_emb_dim * 4`), giving the sinusoidal embedding twice as many frequency components for richer timestep conditioning at minimal parameter cost (~16k extra params in `t_dense1` only).

Added `optax.clip_by_global_norm(1.0)` gradient clipping before AdamW to suppress occasional large gradient spikes during early training, which should improve convergence stability and lower the best validation loss achieved within the fixed epoch budget.

Increased `time_emb_dim` from 128 to 256, doubling the number of sinusoidal frequency components used to encode the timestep before the MLP projection, following the validated pattern that wider sinusoidal embeddings improve timestep conditioning at minimal parameter cost (~32k extra params in `t_dense1` only).

Applied min-SNR-γ weighting (γ=5) to the training loss — each timestep's MSE is scaled by `min(SNR(t), 5) / SNR(t)`, down-weighting easy high-SNR (low-noise) timesteps so the model focuses learning budget on harder, more informative diffusion steps (Hang et al. 2023).

Added a second ResBlock (`mid2: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)`) at the bottleneck immediately after `mid`, doubling the depth of feature processing at 14×14 resolution (~26k extra parameters) to improve the model's capacity to learn noise patterns at the most semantically compressed representation.

Added a second encoder ResBlock (`enc2: ResBlock(C, C, D)`) at 28×28 resolution immediately after `enc1`, doubling the depth of feature processing before downsampling (~26k extra parameters), following the validated pattern that adding depth at the bottleneck improved the model — the same benefit is expected for the full-resolution encoder features used in the skip connection.

Added a second decoder ResBlock (`dec2: ResBlock(C, C, D)`) at 28×28 resolution immediately after `dec1`, symmetric to the `enc2` addition that yielded −0.000343 — decoder depth after the skip-connection merge should improve the model's ability to refine full-resolution features before output (~26k extra parameters).

Added a third bottleneck ResBlock (`mid3: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)`) at the 14×14 resolution immediately after `mid2`, tripling the depth of feature processing at the most semantically compressed representation (~26k extra parameters), following the validated pattern that mid2 gave the largest single depth gain (−0.000526) among the three recent additions.

Added a third encoder ResBlock (`enc3: ResBlock(C, C, D)`) at 28×28 resolution immediately after `enc2`, tripling the encoder depth before downsampling (~26k extra parameters), following the validated pattern that enc2 gave −0.000343 and dec2 gave −0.000419 — richer skip-connection features should continue to benefit decoder reconstruction.

Added a third decoder ResBlock (`dec3: ResBlock(C, C, D)`) at 28×28 resolution immediately after `dec2`, tripling the decoder depth at full resolution (~26k extra parameters) — dec has matched or exceeded enc gains at each step (dec2 gave −0.000419 vs enc3 −0.000295), so symmetric decoder depth should continue to benefit reconstruction quality after the skip-connection merge.

Switched from ε-prediction to v-prediction (`v = √ᾱ·ε − √(1−ᾱ)·x₀`) by changing the training target in `q_sample` and recovering ε from the predicted v in `p_sample_step` — v-prediction balances gradient signal across all noise levels, giving the model a better-conditioned learning objective especially at high-noise timesteps.

Added a skip connection at 14×14 resolution via `enc_14: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)` before the bottleneck and `dec_14: ResBlock(C*4, C*2, D)` after the bottleneck (merging bottleneck output with `enc_14` features), completing a proper U-Net skip at the intermediate resolution — this gives the decoder direct access to pre-bottleneck 14×14 features, analogous to the existing 28×28 skip connection that has consistently improved performance.

Added a second encoder ResBlock at 14×14 resolution (`enc_14_2: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)`) immediately after `enc_14`, deepening the pre-bottleneck features at the intermediate resolution from 1 to 2 layers (~26k extra params) — the 28×28 encoder has 3 layers and each addition there helped, so matching that depth at 14×14 should improve both the skip connection quality and the features fed into the bottleneck.

Added a second decoder ResBlock at 14×14 resolution (`dec_14_2: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)`) immediately after `dec_14`, deepening the post-skip decoder processing at the intermediate resolution from 1 to 2 layers (~26k extra params) — symmetric to `enc_14_2` which gave an unexpectedly large gain (−0.000517), and decoder additions have matched or exceeded encoder additions at every prior step, so this should continue that pattern.

Added a third encoder ResBlock at 14×14 resolution (`enc_14_3: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)`) immediately after `enc_14_2`, deepening the pre-bottleneck encoder from 2 to 3 layers at the intermediate resolution (~26k extra params) — the 28×28 level went to 3 encoder layers with consistently positive returns, and enc_14_2 gave an unexpectedly large gain (−0.000517), so matching 3-layer depth at 14×14 should continue to improve skip connection quality and bottleneck features.

Added a third decoder ResBlock at 14×14 resolution (`dec_14_3: ResBlock(C*2, C*2, D)`) immediately after `dec_14_2`, matching the 3-layer encoder depth at this resolution — decoder additions have matched or exceeded encoder additions at every prior step, and `enc_14_3` gave −0.000271, so symmetric decoder depth should yield a similar or larger gain (~26k extra params).

Introduced a 7×7 resolution level by adding a stride-2 downsample (`down2`) from 14×14, an encoder ResBlock (`enc_7`) with skip connection, moving the existing bottleneck (mid/mid2/mid3) to operate at 7×7 spatial resolution, and a decoder ResBlock (`dec_7`) that merges the skip before upsampling back to 14×14 — adding a new spatial scale (~73k extra params) that should provide higher-leverage feature compression than continuing to deepen 14×14 or 28×28 layers.
